description: Sovereign Cloud & Data Residency reference for GitHub Copilot. Deployment options, data flow classification, provider EU strategies, and residual risks.

sovereignPillars.title: Data Residency
sovereignPillars.description: Control where data is stored and processed geographically. The EU Data Boundary ensures customer data stays within EU/EFTA regions for storage and primary processing. Azure supports region-specific deployments across West Europe, France Central, Sweden Central, and Switzerland North.
sovereignPillars.copilotRelevance: Copilot's residency story is incomplete: GHE.com Data Residency covers repos and CI — but NOT AI traffic. BYO API Key with an EU-region provider is the strongest option for inference residency today.

sovereignPillars.title: Encryption
sovereignPillars.description: Data protection at rest (AES-256, customer-managed keys via HSMs), in transit (TLS 1.2+), and in use (confidential computing with TEEs). Customer-managed keys in external HSMs ensure the cloud provider cannot access plaintext data without explicit customer authorization.
sovereignPillars.copilotRelevance: Copilot prompts are encrypted in transit (TLS). At-rest encryption depends on the provider: Azure OpenAI supports customer-managed keys (CMK); AWS Bedrock uses AWS KMS. In-use encryption via confidential computing is available on Azure OpenAI.

sovereignPillars.title: Confidential Computing
sovereignPillars.description: Hardware-based Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) ensure data is encrypted even during processing. The cloud provider's own operators cannot see the data being processed. Available on Azure via AMD SEV-SNP and Intel TDX enclaves. Complemented by Data Guardian for operational transparency.
sovereignPillars.copilotRelevance: Azure OpenAI with Confidential Computing can process Copilot prompts inside TEEs — the strongest sovereignty guarantee for data in use. Not available on AWS Bedrock or Google Vertex AI today.

deploymentOptions.title: Standard GitHub Copilot
deploymentOptions.description: Default Copilot on github.com with GitHub-managed models. Prompts and completions transit GitHub's US-based API proxy to the model provider. All model providers guarantee zero data retention for GA features, but data crosses EU boundaries during processing.

inMotion.note: Prompts/completions transit US-based GitHub API proxy

inUse.note: Model inference on provider infrastructure (primarily US)

atRest.note: Zero data retention by providers; telemetry stored in US

pros: Simplest deployment — no configuration
Full feature parity across all surfaces
All models available including latest previews
cons: No EU data residency for any Copilot data flow
Schrems II exposure for regulated industries
Telemetry and usage logs in US

deploymentOptions.title: GHE.com with Data Residency
deploymentOptions.description: GitHub Enterprise Cloud on a dedicated GHE.com subdomain with regional data storage (EU, AU, US, JP). Pins repository data, user-generated content, GitHub Actions data, and user metadata to the selected region. Requires Enterprise Managed Users (EMU), SCIM + SAML/OIDC. As of January 2026, Copilot usage metrics and dashboards are available in public preview for EU, AU, US, and JP data residency tenants.
deploymentOptions.criticalInsight: GHE.com Data Residency does NOT cover Copilot prompt and completion traffic. Copilot data is explicitly stored OUTSIDE the selected region. Organizations adopting GHE.com for data residency must not assume Copilot traffic stays in the EU.

inMotion.note: Copilot traffic still routes through US proxy

inUse.note: Model inference remains on US-region providers

atRest.note: Repo data in EU; Copilot telemetry in US

pros: EU residency for repositories, issues, CI/CD
Schrems II compliance for non-AI data
Audit logs and user metadata in region
cons: Copilot data NOT covered — prompts/completions still US
Separate SKU with feature parity gaps

deploymentOptions.title: BYO API Key (Enterprise / Org)
deploymentOptions.description: Enterprise or org admins configure API keys for external providers (Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Google AI Studio, Anthropic, xAI). Copilot routes model inference through your tenant — you control region, retention, and billing. Prompts still pass through GitHub's API proxy for intent detection before reaching your endpoint.

inMotion.note: GitHub proxy hop (US) then your EU endpoint

atRest.note: Retention controlled by your provider tenant

pros: EU inference achievable (Azure, Bedrock, Google)
Full retention and billing control
Centralized key management at enterprise level
cons: Public Preview status — functionality may change
No content filtering guaranteed for BYO models

deploymentOptions.title: BYO API Key (VS Code User)
deploymentOptions.description: Individual developers configure their own API keys in VS Code settings using the Language Models API. Traffic routes directly from the IDE to the provider endpoint, bypassing GitHub's proxy entirely for BYO models. No central governance — each developer manages their own keys.

inMotion.note: Direct IDE to EU endpoint (bypasses GitHub proxy)

inUse.note: Inference on your chosen EU region

pros: No GitHub proxy hop — full EU data path possible
Simplest path to EU inference for individuals
Works with Free/Pro plans (no Enterprise needed)
cons: No central governance — key sprawl risk

deploymentOptions.title: Azure OpenAI / AI Foundry
deploymentOptions.description: Use Azure OpenAI Service or Azure AI Foundry directly — outside the Copilot UX layer — for maximum sovereignty. Full control over region, encryption (CMK), networking (private endpoints), and confidential computing. You lose the Copilot IDE integration, context assembly, and policy enforcement.

inMotion.note: Direct to Azure EU region (private endpoints available)

pros: Full EU sovereignty across all three pillars
Confidential Computing for data in use
Customer-managed encryption keys
Private endpoints and VNet integration
cons: No Copilot UX — must build IDE integration
No GitHub context assembly or policy enforcement

deploymentOptions.title: BYOK via Copilot CLI
deploymentOptions.description: Connect your own Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via Copilot CLI. Run locally via Ollama, vLLM, or Foundry Local. Air-gapped mode available via COPILOT_OFFLINE=true. Shipped April 2026.

inMotion.note: Traffic stays within your own infrastructure when using local models

inUse.note: Local inference — no data leaves the machine

atRest.note: No GitHub-side storage when using local models

pros: Full air-gap capability
Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint
Local model support (Ollama, vLLM)
User-level — no org admin needed
cons: No Copilot Chat features — completions only

residualRisks.title: Intent Detection & Routing
residualRisks.description: GitHub's Copilot proxy inspects prompt fragments for intent classification — routing to the correct surface (Chat, Agent, Completions) and detecting policy violations. This processing occurs on GitHub infrastructure regardless of BYOK or data residency settings. The proxy does not persist prompt content beyond the request lifecycle.
residualRisks.mitigation: Document in your DPIA. Intent detection processes metadata and prompt fragments, not full prompt content. The risk is data transit through US infrastructure, not data storage.

residualRisks.title: Telemetry & Usage Metrics
residualRisks.description: Copilot collects operational telemetry — acceptance rates, latency, feature usage, error rates, token counts. This telemetry flows to GitHub's US infrastructure and may include metadata about prompts (language, file type, suggestion length) but not prompt content itself.
residualRisks.mitigation: Review GitHub's Data Protection Addendum for telemetry scope. Assess whether your data protection framework treats usage metadata as personal data. Telemetry cannot be disabled on Business/Enterprise plans.

residualRisks.title: GHE.com Data Residency Gap
residualRisks.description: GHE.com Data Residency covers repository data, issues, CI/CD, and user metadata — but Copilot prompt and completion traffic is explicitly NOT covered. This is documented in GitHub's data residency documentation. Organizations relying solely on GHE.com for EU compliance have a coverage gap for all AI-powered features.
residualRisks.mitigation: Combine GHE.com DR (for repo data) with BYOK to an EU-region provider (for inference). Document the gap in your DPIA. Monitor GitHub's roadmap — Copilot DR coverage may expand.

providerStrategies.euStrategy: Route through Azure OpenAI Service in EU regions. Azure OpenAI processes data within the EU Data Boundary and supports customer-managed encryption keys. This is the recommended path for EU-compliant GPT inference — the OpenAI direct API does not offer EU region selection.
providerStrategies.byokMethod: Microsoft Foundry provider in GitHub BYOK settings
providerStrategies.zeroRetentionNote: Azure OpenAI: zero retention by default for Copilot. Abuse monitoring opt-out available.

providerStrategies.euStrategy: Route through AWS Bedrock in EU regions. Bedrock guarantees zero data retention and supports EU-central-1 (Frankfurt) and EU-west-1 (Ireland). The Anthropic direct API does not offer explicit EU region selection. Google Vertex AI is an alternative for Claude in europe-west regions.
providerStrategies.byokMethod: AWS Bedrock provider in GitHub BYOK settings
providerStrategies.zeroRetentionNote: AWS Bedrock: zero data retention. Prompts/completions not stored or used for training.

providerStrategies.euStrategy: Route through Google AI Studio or Vertex AI with EU region selection. Vertex AI supports europe-west1 (Belgium) and europe-west4 (Netherlands) with data residency commitments. Google AI Studio: review terms — free-tier usage may be logged.
providerStrategies.byokMethod: Google AI Studio provider in GitHub BYOK settings
providerStrategies.zeroRetentionNote: Vertex AI: zero retention for paid API calls. Google AI Studio free tier may log — verify current terms.

providerStrategies.euStrategy: No EU region available. xAI infrastructure is US-only. Not suitable for organizations with EU data residency requirements. Consider alternative models for EU-regulated workloads.
providerStrategies.byokMethod: Not applicable for EU use cases
providerStrategies.zeroRetentionNote: xAI states zero retention (RAM-only processing). Verify current terms.

dataFlowDiagram:
flowchart LR
    subgraph IDE["Developer IDE"]
        P["Prompt + Context"]
    end
    subgraph GH["GitHub Proxy"]
        ID["Intent Detection\nRouting · Telemetry"]
    end
    subgraph STD["Standard Path"]
        M["Model Provider\n(US regions)"]
    end
    subgraph BYOK_PATH["BYOK Path"]
        BM["Your Endpoint\n(EU region possible)"]
    end
    P -->|"TLS 1.2+"| ID
    ID -->|"Standard"| M
    ID -.->|"BYOK"| BM
    M -->|"Completion"| P
    BM -.->|"Completion"| P
    style IDE fill:#0d2818,stroke:#00ff88,color:#00ff88
    style GH fill:#2a1a00,stroke:#ffaa00,color:#ffaa00
    style STD fill:#1a0d2e,stroke:#aa88ff,color:#aa88ff
    style BYOK_PATH fill:#001a0d,stroke:#00ff88,color:#00ff88
